Tom Nu:r:sall & Harris Goldberg Based on the story by Ken Johnson Based on Characters Created by Steven Brill GORDON, THE BOY ON THE LAKE OF THE ISLES POND, skates alone, happy. The following should overlap and echo to create a dream-like montage. GORDON puts a hand on CHARLIE'S shoulder.
Look around. Who'd of thought we'd come this far? Triple deke and you got 'em.
Thanks coach.
CHARLIE scores the goal. The DUCKS go mad with celebration.
*FLASHBACK - FINAL GAME DUCKS II
The full stadium rocks. THE DUCKS are hit hard, losing. see Gordon on the bench and hear ...
We're not goons, we're not bullies. You, who are you?
As the voices echo, "Dean Portman, Chicago, Illinois, Connie Moreau Minneapolis, Minnesota ... etc. A series of overlapping dissolves shows us brief shots of the faces of our kids.
Russ Tyler, South Central Los Angeles
Charlie Conway, Minneapolis Minnesota.
We see FULTON'S shot that knocks the Iceland goalie over, we see JULIE make her game winning save. And we see the DUCKS win and go crazy. GORDON in the middle.
INT. AIRPORT - NORTHWEST TERMINAL - FLASHBACK

A news conference is under way. GORDON and DUCKS listen as DEAN FRANKLIN BUCKLEY makes a speech for the press. Buckley"s warm tone suggests sincerity, but in reality he"s smooth and slippery ... the perfect politician. Among the crowd we see the proud local parents, especially a beaming CASEY. In the b.g., we note a not-so-happy THOM PORTER, President of the Varsity Parents' Booster Club.

(looking at the Ducks) And so, it's my great pleasure to award you these full athletic scholarships to the Westbrook Academy, where we hope the Ducks will be happy Warriors under the leadership of The Minnesota Miracle Man himself, and former alumni ... Gordon Bombay.
APPLAUSE. CHARLIE looks over to CASEY, then up to GORDON. GORDON looks surprised.
AIRPORT - LATER
GORDON corners BUCKLEY.
I can't accept the job. You knew that.
(instantly apologetic) Sorry, Gordon. I let my enthusiasm get the better of me.
GORDON nods, looks to the DUCKS and CHARLIE.
We're gonna rule in high school!
EXT. LAKE OF THE ISLES POND - FALL -- PRESENT TIME:

GORDON BOMBAY sitting on a park bench near the now unfrozen pond. It's fall and leaves drop from the trees. Some real ducks make circles near the edge. GORDON looks at them. In his hand he holds a letter. Suddenly something flies over his head, GORDON jumps a bit. It's CHARLIE on ROLLERBLADES leaping over the back of the bench and skating-before him. He does a spinning move -(CONTINUED) "spinorama" -- shoots an imaginary puck, scores, sits down right next to him.
You rang, master.
Where did you learn that?
Spinorama? I've been working on it all swmner. I figure they know I got the triple deke, let's show 'em something new, Coach.
( looking down.} I'm not going to be your coach this year.
Very funny.
I got the official word today. (re: letter) The Junior Goodwill Committee has named me the director of player personnel. I'll be in charge of their junior hockey program worldwide.
{desperate) You could do both GORDON. (shaking his head) I'll be on the road all the time.

So that's it? You stick us in some stupid fancy school and then GORDON Chalie, listen to me. What the Ducks need now is a coach who can take you to the next level. What I do best ... are little ducks. Do you understand? CHARLIE'S up, near tears. Can't believe this.
Yeah. You're skating on me just like my old man. Do me a favor. Don't send me a birthday card.
Come on, Charlie. I don't deserve that. (gently) The good news is, I think I found you a terrific coach.
I don't want a new coach!

CHARLIE moves off quickly down the path, plowing right through a DUCK crossing, sending a single mallard off in the air, away from CHARLIE, GORDON and the POND. THE MALLARD Becomes a speck in the sky.
EXT. AIRPLANE - TOUCHES DOWN INT. AIRPORT - AUSTIN GATE
DWAYNE comes striding off. Two small suitcases in hand, wearing a cowboy hat and a huge smile.
Howdy, Minnesota. Great to be back.
ANGELA DELVECHIO appears on an airport tram. She wears a name tag that identifies her as a member of the Westbrook Faculty. Twenty-something, with a killer smile, she's every high school boy's dream.
You must be Dwayne.
How'd ya guess?
INT. LOS ANGELES GATE
RUSS is causing a ruckus. He holds his head-set as he leaves the jet-way, a surly STEWARD chases after him .
Sir, you may not keep the headsets.
I paid four dollars for this licorice stick with q-tips, and all I got was the
Kenny Gaudio channel and a Michael J.
It was a rental.
I'm extending the rental period. (sees COWBOY) Cowboy! Howdeee, Partner! (gives headset back) Here, I was just messin' with ya!
He jumps on the Tram and they wiz off. We see a couple kissing passionately. They separate. It's LUIS and a PRETTY GIRL. The girl is tearful. LUIS smiles, * wipes her tears away.
Tu siempre eres mi corozon. I'll see you again.
He looks up and sees* the TRAM. He smiles and jumps on.
Man, Luis, who was that?
(all eyes on Angela) A 9irl I met on the plane .
.ANOTHER GATE - BANGOR, MAINE
JULIE exits her plane loaded with carry-ens. help her. The luggage drops to the floor. A MAN tries to
What do you have in here, weights?
Yup .
HEY CATLADY!

She picks up the bag, looks up to see the tram with her friends on it. She jumps on. They ride along, HAPPY, singing "We will rock you!" Angela joins in on the song. SAN FRANCISCO GATE -- KEN WU runs gracefully towards the moving tram. With one fantastic leap, he lands beautifully on the back. The Team pulls him in and they are off.
FULTON I s ALLEY - DAY
FULTON and CHARLIE hit pucks into an open suitcase. FULTON misses and sends the puck out of the alley. We hear the screech of tires.
it.
No Portman! I can"t believe
When he he~rd Bombay was out, he stayed in Chicago. Everyone else came though.*
CHARLIE shoots, nails one. He's been practicing.
How could he break up the bash brothers?
Tell me about it, man.
How about calling me, Mr. Bash, or "The Bashman? " . . .. Nah. . .
Frustrated, he shoots another wild shot that ricochets off something and breaks glass somewhere.
No bash brothers, no Gordon, a school

year is really gonna suck. In anger they both wind up and shoot, we hear the sound of breaking glass, a car screeching, a cat shriek, and a person's scream. They cringe. Pause. AVERMAN and JESSE on rollerblades, turn the corner. AVERMAN holds his head with one hand and an errant puck with the other.
Don't shoot, don't shoot.
It's okay, you just hit me in the head. It's not like I need it for school or anything. (they laugh a little} You ever think about shooting back the other way?
He shrugs, "no. " the alley, it certainly would make sense.
Come on, let's go meet up with the rest. First day of school, we gotta be on time.
EXT; BAND BOX RESTAURANT.;. DAY
Ready to skate, GOLDBERG, CONNIE and GUY are waiting. CONNIE & GUY however are fighting. GOLDBERG is between them.
You tell him that I don't want him in any of my classes.
Guy, Connie apparently thinks ...
She broke up with me. I don't want to be in her clas*ses either. Tell her ...
I'm not a licensed therapist.
And another thing.
I don't want to hear it.
Come on you two ... (CONT:'.:NUED)
GUY&. CONNIE Stay out of it, Goldber~!
It's gonna be a great year.
AVERMAN, JESSE, CHARLIE, FULTON skate up, quacking. They high-five.
Alright, Ducks let's move out. We're picking up Banks on the way.
Why do we have to skate to school the first day? I'll be all sweaty and gross.
Ducks fly together.
EXT. BANKS' HOUSE - UPPER CLASS - DAY
ALL the DUCKS plow into his yard and start quacking. JESSE Come on out, cake-eater, let's go to school. BANKS is dressed all preppy, on blades. The KIDS LAUGH.
Nice tie. Where you goin a funeral?
(yanks it, it isn't) Whoops, sorryi BANKS
Hey, I care about the way I look, what's
Nothin' Banksie. Let us help you.
CHARLIE, AVERMAN and GOLDBERG attack him, mess up his hair, pull out his shirt.
There, much better .
BANKS' anger turns into a smile, he's a duck.
I've devised the perfect shortcut. Follow me.
They all skate off, except for BANKS who remains in place with arms folded. Soon, AVERMAN leads the flock back the other way. Which way, Columbus?
They skate off. BANKS falls in line.
EXT. WESTBROOK SCHOOL
The preppy student body walks to class. The Dormers: JULIE, WU, LUIS; RUSS and DWAYNE walk to the auditorium feeling like immigrants.
I want to know who this new coach Orion is. My Dad said, if I didn't like him I could come right home.
That's nice. My Dad said I better stay in on scholarship or he'd whip my butt.
I heard in the dorm the dude played for the Minnesota North Stars.
Dallas Stars now, pardner. (proud) We done rustled us a hockey team . . JULIE (whispering) I wouldn't brag about that up here.
Look Cowboy, Indians. A PICKET LINE Sure enough a GROUP OF STUDENTS, and. some NATIVE AMERICANS,

and STUDENT ACTIVISTS are picketing. SIGNS read "NATIVE AMERICANS ARE PEOPLE NOT WARRIORS." THE WESTBROOK symbol of the Indian warrior has a circle and lin.e through it. And we see LINDA JEFFRIES, a cute, young freshman with a confidence that far outstrips her years. Her vintage clothing and committed attitude marks her as anything but a spoiled preppy.
Who're they?
Ain"t you never seen a protest?
You bet I have! One time back home they said we couldn't spray the horseflies? Man, that sure 'nuff kicked up some dust!
A PREPPY overhears this and turns to a friend: PREPPY Where'd they find these guys?
(turning) I heard that!
EXT. STREETS
The DUCKS fly in the V down the street. Looking cool. AVERMAN moves to lead.point, adjust his glasses and checks a MAP; he directs them to the right.
DOWN A SIDE STREET

They fly and head right down a STEEP HILL. They pick up way too much speed, too fast to stop. GOLDBERG teeters. GOLDBERG I'm not a skater ... I'm just a goalieeee! GOLDBERG regains his balance, but this time, he is going backward, panicked ... out of control. GOLDBERG Out of my way, Goalie comin' through. He flies by the rest of the pack, backwards. The DUCKS look at each other. CHARLIE peels off like a jet fighter and goes after the stray . ON THE HILL CHARLIE closes on him. We see at the bottom the hill a huge lake. A small fishing pier juts out. GOLDBERG is flailing backwards, CHARLIE IS right on him. Gaining. He reaches for him. Just misses. AT THE
BOTI'OM

The road turns, GOLDBERG does not. GOLDBERG rockets out onto the pier, CHARLIE right on him. They pass startled old fishermen ... GOLDBERG'S about to go right off the end, when CHARLIE grabs him by the belt buckle, puts on the brakes and with great effort stops him from going into the lake. GOLDBERG'S back wheels are just off the pier. breath. A beat.
Thanks Charlie.
They 're about to move off ... OLD MAN ( V. 0 . )
An OLD FISHERMAN yanks hard on his pole, the fish flies out of the water and smashes into CHARLIE'S face, sending hi:m right into the water.
INT. ASSEMBLY HALL - DAY

The fabulously preppy students watch as the DUCKS move to their seats. Behind them, RICK, the varsity captain, appears. He has MINDY, the head cheerleader with him. And COLE, his enforcer thug. Other VARSITY THUGS hover nearby.
You don't belong at Westbrook. We don't need you ruining our good name.
I'll give ya a good name.
Otto.
Otto?
Yeah, outto my face before I knock you into the next grade.
Oh, we got us a real tough homeboy.
I ain't your homeboy, punk.
Easy Russ. They must think we're someone else.
Meanwhile, LUIS is making serious eye contact with MINDY. Who despite it all, kinda digs him back.
I'm Dwayne, we're the new hockey team.
The only hockey team. Varsity. State Champs. My little brother lost his JV slot when they brought you yo-yo's in here.
Probably wasn't good enough. Intrigued SCO'IT, the Varsity goalie, locks ~yes with her. She
stares defiantly.
Yeah, well we 1 ll see who 1 s good enough when we kick your little duck butts back to the swamp you crawled out of.
Where's the rest *of you pukes?
Pukes? That one of your preppy put downs? (pulling out a notepad and writing it down; sarcastic) Man, I got to get that down. That is bad.
EXT. BO'ITOM OF HILL NEAR LAKE OF THE ISLES - DAY

FULTON has accidentally knocked into BANKS. CHARLIE, wet but determined, races along with the rest. They are not sure where they are. The road dead ends into thick woods. They look at Averman, who consults the map. BANKS Nice shortcut.
These trees shouldn't be here.
INT. AUDITORIUM
Proceedings are about to begin. podium. DEAN BUCKLEY stands at the
"LUX ESTO! II
What'd he say?
.* ( looking at Dwayne like he' s dumb) LUX ESTO!
DWAYNE nods, "Oh.*
Let there be light!* (beat) Since 1903, the headmaster has commenced the school year with those inspiring words.
He looks good for a man that age. WU, JULIE, LUIS and others crack up.
Yes, we cherish our tradition at the Westbrook Academy ... but we also recognize the need for change. And so today, we are proud to open our doors to a truly gifted group of student athletes ...
EXT. ROAD
THE EXHAUSTED DUCKS turn a corner on the road and stumble upon the gates of WESTBROOK.
I told you I knew where I was going.
They give him dirty looks.
Come on, we can make it.

Suddenly four horses, rode by proper ENGLISH RIDERS, run in front of them. CHARLIE almost gets trampled. They look up at them as they pass by without a look. It's the equestrian team. CHARLIE looks down just in time to sidestep a pile of horse manure.
INT. AUDITORIUM
The DEAN Presses on.
Will you join me i~ a big "Warrior Welcome" for LINDA (shouting out) "Warriors" is offensive to all Native .Americans.
(quieting the stir) This is a serious issue that I personally have recommended for consideration to the Board of Directors.
Where do you stand on the issue, Dean
I stand for tolerance and respect ... whichever way the Board rules. LINDA and others boo.
THE VARSITY turns to.quiet them. PROTESTERS march out.
EXT. SCHOOL - THE DUCKS
Racing to auditorium. They try a side door, it's locked. They race around to what they think is a front door. It's open. They hear the DEAN inside. CHARLIE quiets them.
We'll slip in the back row. notice.
No one will
INT. AUDITORIUM - SAME TIME

The door opens into darkness. CHARLIE leads them in. They take one step into darkness and find themselves flying down a set of stairs. They are backstage. DEAN BUCKLEY ... the 1991 Minnesota State Pee-Wee champions ... the gold medal winners of the Junior Goodwill Games ... I present to youCHARLIE WHOOOAAA! CHARLIE, followed by the rest of the out of control DUCKS fly right onto the stage and wipe out behind the DEAN. GOLDBERG is about.to crash into him, when he grabs the curtain, yanking it down with a mighty rip. It completely covers everyone. SILENCE. ALL in the audience are aghast, especially THOM PORTER, the hockey Booster Club President. DEAN BUCKLEY stares. CHARLIE'S head peeks out.

Hi, We're the Ducks. EXT. DEAN BUCKLEY OFFICE - THE DUCKS ARE GATHERED A large, tastefully furnished office. The DEAN'S DEGREES hang on the .walls *as do SEVERAL PHOTOGRAPHS of the DEAN with various VIPS. We note one *of him shaking hands* with WALTER MONDALE. There's one curiosity in the corner that immediately catches the DUCKS' attention -- a GIANT ANT FARM in a huge aquarium. Red ants and sand.
Check this out. Antsville.
Look at 'em go.
You can learn a lot from ants .
Everyone looks up startled. BUCKLEY enters.
An amazing species, Brazilian fire ants. One queen. The rest are workers. Everybody pulls their weight and nobody complains. Wouldn't it be nice if things ran so smoothly for the rest of us?
Nice for who?
The queen or the workers?
Stifled laughs. BUCKLEY grins at Charlie, then balances himself on the edge of his desk.

Kids, I need your help here. Qu'ite frankly, bringing you all in was a bit of a risk. We've got a group of Booster Club Parents whose feathers are a little ruffled -- if you'll pardon the pun. You see, they think that because they pay, their kids should get to play. So, what we need is for you to keep up those grades and keep winning those games. That way, we're all covered. I just want you to remember I 'm on your team. Any questions?
Is it true, sir -- Terrible Ted Orion is going to be our coach?
(smiling and nodding) It was quite a coup to get him. AVERMAN They say nobody picked a fight with him ... if they wanted to live.
(winking) That's what they say. (checking his watch) Time for your first class.
INT. MATH CLASS - MONTAGE
WU, RUSS, GOLDBERG, CONNIE, GUY, CHARLIE, stare as MRS. MADIGAN explains her rules .

Every Monday, you will have a practice quiz. Every Wednesday you will have a real quiz. Every Friday you will have an exam. And every time I feel like it you will have a surprise quiz or exam. There will be no calculators or modern calculating devices allowed. You will only have the use of a number 2 pencil ...
She continues on as we see the horrified look of our kids.
INT. MR. BARBER'S HISTORY
JULIE, AVERMAN, FULTON, BANKS and others watch MR. BARBER, a black teacher with a booming voice, as he "demonstrates" history with the aid of two dolls, a giant and a dwarf.
It"s been said that the present is to the
giant. If the dwarf holds his seat, he can indeeed see further than the giant.
The class is spellbound and scared.
INT. ANGELA DELVECHIO'S ENGLISH CLASS
She is just starting to take attendance.
(sheepish) You can me Averman.
(nodding) I used to hate the sound of my own name too.* An-gel-a. And I'll tell you a little secret. When I was 14? I changed my name. Well, actually, I just started (MORE)

using my middle name -- Mary. Now, hey, there's a nice uncommon name -- Mary! No more Angela or Angel for me, boy. Just please-don't call~attention-to-me-GodMary! (beat) Then, one day, a new girl comes to school. All the guys drop dead over her. What's her name? You guessed it! Angela! Wait a minute, that's my name!
Everybody LAUGHS.

You know something, Lester Averrnan? Some of the coolest characters ever have some really strange names. Huckleberry Finn. Hester Prynne. Holden Caulfield. The Great Gatsby. You're going to meet all of them in here. And you're going to see their names are cool because they are cool -- just like you.
LESTER AVER.MAN smiles.
INT. SCHOOL HALLWAY - BETWEEN CLASSES
LUIZ looks at MINDY and starts following*her. He is body checked hard into a locker by COLE. WE PICK UP other DUCKS walking the other way.
Homework on the first day. I don't believe it.
I know we better be careful or we're gonna learn something here.
You see the computer lab. This place is state of the art.
I'm gonna take band. Down home I used to fiddle some with the sax.
Cowboy, how are you going to learn French when you murder English?
INT. HALLWAY - SAME TIME
CHARLIE is a little lost, looking for his next class. He sees LINDA, the girl from the picket line. She is trying, with no luck, to get students to sign her petition. CHARLIE is instantly smitten.
Hi, will you sign a petition
Sure, what's it for?
We're demanding the Board change the demeaning Warrior name.
(about to sign) Warriors isn't so bad, is it? I mean, you got the Indians, the Braves, the Hawks, the Redskins LINDA You're a jock, aren't you?
(puffing up) Yeah, I play hockey. I was captain of LINDA (pulling back her petition) Forget it. All you Warrior jocks stick together.
She walks away. CHARLIE calls after her.
I'm not a Warrior. I'm a duck.
WEIRD DAVE, a kind.of stoner guy looks at him.
And I'm a rooster, Man. doodle dooooo!
Watch. CHARLIE looks at this strange guy and moves on.
THE STUDENT DINING ROOM - LUNCH HOUR

RICK, COLE and the VARSITY are eating their lunch in this classy wood-paneled, nicely carpeted dining hall. Captain RICK holds court. We see LUIS on the periphery winking at MINDY, who can't believe his arrogance and nerve. She doesn't bust him though.
They don't belong here. My Dad says it was just a publicity stunt, but it's gonna wind up killing the school's reputation.
Yeah, them rejects shoulda stayed on their side of the tracks.
SCOTT, the varsity goalie, isn't a jerk.
Those rejects, genius. And besides we don't have tracks, Cole. There are no trains, therefore no tracks, get it?
Well, they should stay out of our~ school.
Don't get smart with me, goalie! COLE snarls at SCOTI'. RICK steps in jokingly.
Come on, Scooter, you know better than to confuse, Cole. We're on the same team.
CHARLIE, brown bag in hand, wanders past their table. WU is with him.
Well, look at captain duckie ... He grabs CHARLIE'S bag and tosses it to COLE. SCOTI' moves away, disgusted. COLE'S happy that he has someone to pick on.
Hey, give me that ...
(pulling out a Twinkie) Oh, look, Mommy packed a Twinkie.
CHARLIE tries to grab it away. COLE who is about to eat it. RICK tosses the Twinkie to
I wouldn't eat that.
But I'm not you, am I?
COLE smiles maliciously and gulps it in one bite. He spits it out immediately, some of it landing on RICK.
Eeechh, it's all wet.
That'll happen when you fall in a lake.
and CHARLIE crack up. FULTON and RUSS show up. COLE moves to grab him. LUIS jumps in too. Just then
Get your hands off him.
Ohhh, look a bash brother. I'm sooo scared.
They get right into it. It could turn into a brawl, even though the DUCKS are outnumbered. But, DEAN BUCKLEY steps right in.
Well, well, I see the two hockey teams are making fast friends. Rick, are you extending every courtesy to our new
student~athletes?
Yes, Dean Buckley. Charlie is our
Throws a rough arm around him.
Oh yeah, buddies for life.
THE WESTBROOK RINK - AFTER SCHOOL

Against a background of GREEN, a RED Indian head stares straight at us above two crossed Indian tomahawks. VARSITY CHAMPIONSHIP banners hang from the ceilings. ON THE side wall is a huge, long display case of hockey trophies and medals and pictures. The DUCKS (J.V.) in full gear look at the case. RUSS So, what, they got trophies. I don't see no Goodwill Gold Medals.
No, but look at this.
INSERT - GORDON Captain of the Debate Team, making a fevered point. GOLDBERG Captain of the debate team, Gordon
Look at that haircut. It's Greg Brady. They all take a moment to look, a little nostalgic.
I miss him already. I wish he was here.
Yeah, well, he's not is he? Suddenly, we HEAR A LOUD YE-HAW!

EM UP'*. DOGGIES! Everyone looks at each other, breaks out in smiles and runs for the ice. Here comes Cowboy DWAYNE with his LASSO. Everybody scatters as DWAYNE whips his lasso and charges forward. CHARLIE narrowly escapes the first throw by executing a great spinorama ... THE LASSO falls inches short. EVERYONE' S flying around the ice, WHOOPING and LAUGHING, having a blast as they play their special brand of tag. We see CHARLIE helping FULTON and RUSS skate, encouraging them. DWAYNE zeroes in on RUSS. He throws the lasso and at the last minute CHARLIE pushes RUSS out of the way. Unfortunately, it encircles CHARLIE and pulls him to the ice. CLOSE ON CHARLIE On his side, he feels like a hopeless calf ... then HEARS THE SHRILL BLAST OF A WHISTLE. CHARLIE'S POV Moving towards him are TWO LARGE SKATES. His gaze moves up to reveal his new coach, TED ORION. Wonderfully weathered for a man still in his thirties, he seems larger than life. There's an air about him that radiates authority. It starts with his intense gaze. He leans down to untie Charlie.
My name
is Coach Orion. You may call me "Coach" or "Coach Orion."
(testing the waters) You can call me Charlie.
The FRESHMEN stifle their laughs.
(fixing his gaze on Charlie) I tell you what, Charlie. You give me
CHARLIE flips over ~d starts doing push-ups.
(blowing his whistle) I meant laps ! Go! Now!
CHARLIE takes off, the OTHERS take note. The COACH starts tightly wrapping Dwayne's rope around his hand like a noose.
Now listen up. Listen good. We are here f o r ~ reason -- and one reason only ... (staring at AVERMAN) Do you know what that is? (AVERMAN is at a loss.) I'll give you a hint. It starts with a
To win!
No. To work! High school hockey is work. Learning to play hockey the way it was meant to be played is very hard work. And it all begins with defense. (beat) I've seen your tapes. I know you can
I just don't know that you can stop them.
(taking offense) Hey (ORION stops him with a stare.) Sorry, Coach. Coach Orion. Sir.
So, here's the deal. If you want to play in my barn, you better be ready to work your tails off ... at both ends. Is that clear? Count off!
The *ONE'S" (GOLDBERG, DWAYNE, BANKS, JESSE, GUY, and WU) line up behind the goal line in start position. The COACH stands a few feet in front of them, whistle and stopwatch ready.
Conway, you're dogging it. _You owe me another ten. Everyone else. Let's climb the mountain.
He WHISTLES, off they go. conditioning/sprint drill.
"Climb the Mountain" is a killer

We see that BANKS and DWAYNE quickly skate away from the pack. Others struggle and tire quickly. CHARLIE, out of breath, finishes his laps and falls in behind AVERMAN, who waits for the "two" whistle. ORION'S keen eye seems to evaluate everything in an instant.
This guy was an All-Star ...
For one season, didn't he wash out? disappeared .
No one knew why. He just
Well, here he is ... just our luck.
What's that, Conway?
I was just saying what a nice change it
is to skate instead of practice the game.
If I want your color commentary, I'll ask for it.
SHOOTING DRILL
The One's take turns shooting at GOLDBERG. BANKS beats him clean. So does DWAYNE. So does FULTON. Everyone seems to beat him -- except CHARLIE, who makes one move too many.
Triple deke, Conway? Save the fancy
moves for the dance floor. At the other end of the ice, JULIE .appears to_be stopping the Two's cold.
STICKHANDLING DRILL
DWAYNE is a whiz as he leads the way around and through the cones, with pucks. BANKS is equally smooth.* CHARLIE cuts a cone too closely and trips. He gets up, determined, all heart.
This is why we drill, Conway.
Why are you picking on me?
The COACH skates off, deliberately not hearing the question.
Bombay never did any of this stuff.
Bombay never played pro ... CHARLIE stares off at the new guy with intense dislike. LATER COACH d~s a puck bag in front of FULTON, RUSS, CHARLIE.
Okay hot shots, let's see it. Let's see
With pleasure.
ORION moves about twenty feet between FULTON and the net.
I wouldn't stand there.
Thanks for the warning.
(under his breath) Let it rip, Fulton.

FULTON unwinds a blistering slapshot. knees and easily smothers the puck. The COACH drops to his FULTON is stunned. ORION waves him on. FULTON shoots another. ORION blocks it easily. He shoots a barrage. ORION blocks them.all. The net remains empty. RUSS steps in and winds up his knucklepuck and amazingly, ORION swats it out of the air with his glove like it was a fly. Then, he skates up to CHARLIE and slides him the puck.
Lead the charge, Conway.
Three on one?
Many as you need.

CHARLIE circles at center ice, motions for BANKS, CONNIE and AVERMAN to join him, RUSS and FULTON. They attack six on one. ORION assumes his perfect defensive stance inside his blue line. Without a trace of panic, he effortlessly follows the play, managing to hold them off at every turn.
